fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/src/validators/order.validator.ts b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..fef86d4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+/**
+ * Order validators — pure validation functions for order operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ */
+
+import type { Order, OrderItem, OrderStatus } from '../models/order.js';
+
+/**
+ * Allowed status transitions for orders.
+ *
+ * Each key maps to the list of statuses it can transition to.
+ */
+const STATUS_TRANSITIONS: Record<OrderStatus, OrderStatus[]> = {
+  pending: ['confirmed', 'cancelled'],
+  confirmed: ['shipped', 'cancelled'],
+  shipped: ['delivered'],
+  delivered: [],
+  cancelled: [],
+};
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an order exists.
+ *
+ * @param id - The order ID that was looked up.
+ * @param order - The result of the lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the order is not found.
+ */
+export function validateOrderExists(
+  id: string,
+  order: Order | undefined,
+): asserts order is Order {
+  if (!order) {
+    throw new Error(`Order not found: ${id}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an order has at least one item.
+ *
+ * @param items - The order items array.
+ * @throws Error if the items array is empty.
+ */
+export function validateOrderHasItems(items: OrderItem[]): void {
+  if (items.length === 0) {
+    throw new Error('Order must have at least one item');
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Check whether a status transition is valid.
+ *
+ * Pure predicate — does not throw.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @returns true if the transition is allowed, false otherwise.
+ */
+export function isValidStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): boolean {
+  return STATUS_TRANSITIONS[from].includes(to);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that a status transition is allowed.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @throws Error if the transition is not allowed.
+ */
+export function validateStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): void {
+  if (!isValidStatusTransition(from, to)) {
+    throw new Error(
+      `Invalid status transition: ${from} -> ${to}`,
+    );
+  }
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..2dd43d5
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+/**
+ * Preferences validators — pure validation functions for user preference operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ */
+
+import type { UserNotificationPreferences } from '../models/user-preferences.js';
+
+/**
+ * Validate that preferences do not already exist for a user.
+ *
+ * Used during preference creation to prevent duplicates.
+ *
+ * @param userId - The user ID being checked.
+ * @param existing - Existing preferences for the user (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if preferences already exist for the user.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesNotExist(
+  userId: string,
+  existing: UserNotificationPreferences | undefined,
+): void {
+  if (existing) {
+    throw new Error(`Preferences already exist for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that preferences exist for a user.
+ *
+ * Used during preference updates and queries that require existing preferences.
+ *
+ * @param userId - The user ID being checked.
+ * @param existing - Existing preferences for the user (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if no preferences are found for the user.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesExist(
+  userId: string,
+  existing: UserNotificationPreferences | undefined,
+): asserts existing is UserNotificationPreferences {
+  if (!existing) {
+    throw new Error(`No preferences found for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/user.validator.ts b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ce3fe76
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+/**
+ * User validators — pure validation functions for user operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ *
+ * Reusable across services (e.g. OrderService validates user existence
+ * before creating an order).
+ */
+
+import type { User } from '../models/user.js';
+
+/**
+ * Validate that a user exists.
+ *
+ * @param id - The user ID that was looked up.
+ * @param user - The result of the lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @throws Error if user is not found.
+ */
+export function validateUserExists(
+  id: string,
+  user: User | undefined,
+): asserts user is User {
+  if (!user) {
+    throw new Error(`User not found: ${id}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is not already taken.
+ *
+ * Used during user creation to enforce email uniqueness.
+ *
+ * @param email - The email to check.
+ * @param existingUser - A user already using that email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the email is already in use.
+ */
+export function validateEmailNotTaken(
+  email: string,
+  existingUser: User | undefined,
+): void {
+  if (existingUser) {
+    throw new Error(`User with email ${email} already exists`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is available for a user update.
+ *
+ * Only checks uniqueness when the email is actually being changed.
+ *
+ * @param newEmail - The new email (may be undefined if not changing).
+ * @param currentEmail - The user's current email.
+ * @param existingUserWithEmail - A user already using the new email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the new email is already in use by another user.
+ */
+export function validateEmailAvailableForUpdate(
+  newEmail: string | undefined,
+  currentEmail: string,
+  existingUserWithEmail: User | undefined,
+): void {
+  if (newEmail && newEmail !== currentEmail && existingUserWithEmail) {
+    throw new Error(`Email ${newEmail} is already in use`);
+  }
+}

prompt:
You are Agent 5 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-gIRIAm.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Extract validation logic from service classes into dedicated validator modules.

Specifically:
1. Review the services in src/services/ to identify inline validation logic.
2. Create a src/validators/ directory with validator modules for each service that has inline validation.
3. Validators should be pure functions — no side effects, no database calls.
4. Update services to call validators instead of doing inline validation.
5. Add tests for the new validators.
6. Make sure all existing tests still pass after the refactoring.

Important:
- This is a refactoring — existing behavior must not change.
- Validators should be reusable across services.
- Follow existing code style and patterns.

parameters.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-gIRIAm thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The full directory structure (all files)
2. All service files in src/services/ - read each one completely
3. Any existing validators in src/validators/
4. All model/interface definitions in src/models/
5. All test files in tests/
6. The utils directory contents
7. Any config files (tsconfig.json, package.json, etc.)

Read every file completely - I need the full picture of the codebase.

Get src directory structure with TS files
